input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?
input_dict.current_output: Neither is clearly more diverse — both have comparably varied careers. Jan Gustaf Troell usually writes his own scripts, serves as his own director of photography, and directs films. Jason Kent Bateman has worked as an actor, director, and producer.

upstream_context.question: Between Jason Bateman and Jan Troell, which filmmaker has had a more diverse career?
upstream_context.summary_1:
Primary topics: Jan Troell (Jan Gustaf Troell) and Jason Bateman (Jason Kent Bateman)

Facts supported by passages:
- Jan Troell: "Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931)"; identified as a "Swedish film director"; "Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography." Directed "Hurricane" (1979) (film directed by Jan Troell). Directed "Everlasting Moments" (2008). Made "Land of Dreams" (1988), original Swedish title "Sagolandet"; filming took place "from 1983 to 1986."
- Jason Bateman: "Jason Kent Bateman (born January 14, 1969)"; identified as an "American actor, director, and producer." Began acting on television "in the early 1980s" on "Little House on the Prairie", and sitcoms "Silver Spoons" and "The Hogan Family." Known for role Michael Bluth in "Arrested Development" (won a Golden Globe and a Satellite Award). Has starring film roles in many films and the 2017 Netflix series "Ozark."

Ambiguities / missing facts blocking a direct comparison:
- "More diverse career" is undefined (no metric supplied). Missing: a comparable list/count of distinct occupations/roles and types of credits for each person (e.g., director, screenwriter, cinematographer, producer, actor, voice actor, television vs film directing/producing).
- Candidate name variants: "Jan Troell" vs "Jan Gustaf Troell" (same person); "Jason Bateman" vs "Jason Kent Bateman" (same person).
- Missing bridge facts to compare diversity: full filmography/credit list for Troell showing roles per film (director, writer, cinematographer) and Bateman's directing/producing credits beyond acting.

Prioritized next-hop retrievals:
1. "Jan Troell" — fetch Wikipedia page; inspect lede/infobox, "Career", and "Filmography" sections for complete list of occupations and credit roles (director/writer/cinematographer) and genres.
2. "Jason Bateman" — fetch Wikipedia page; inspect lede/infobox, "Career", and "Filmography" sections for full list of occupations, directing/producing credits, voice work, and TV vs film roles.
3. "Jan Troell filmography" (or the "Filmography" section of Troell's page) — extract role-by-role credits (who he served as cinematographer, writer, director, etc.).
4. Search keywords if needed: "Jan Gustaf Troell occupations cinematographer screenwriter photographer" and "Jason Kent Bateman directing credits producer credits voice acting" to capture any roles not listed on the main pages.
upstream_context.hop2_query: How do Jason Bateman and Jan Troell careers differ occupations roles
hop2_titles: How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love?
hop2_docs: Hurricane (1979 film) | Hurricane is a 1979 romance and adventure film featuring Mia Farrow and Jason Robards, produced by Dino De Laurentiis and Lorenzo Semple, Jr., and directed by Jan Troell.  It is loosely based on the 1937 film of the same name.

Jan Troell | Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.  Usually, Troell writes his own scripts and serves as his own director of photography.  His realistic films, with a lyrical photography in which nature is prominent, have placed him in the first rank of modern Swedish film directors along with Ingmar Bergman and Bo Widerberg.

Breaking the Rules (film) | Breaking the Rules is a 1992 American drama film directed by Neal Israel, executive produced by Larry A. Thompson, starring Jason Bateman, C. Thomas Howell, Jonathan Silverman and Annie Potts.  Jason's father, Kent Bateman, has a role in the movie as well.

Hamsun (film) | Hamsun is a 1996 internationally co-produced drama film directed by Jan Troell, about the later life of the Norwegian author Knut Hamsun (Max von Sydow), who, together with his wife Marie Hamsun (Ghita Nørby), went from being a national hero to a traitor after supporting Nazi Germany during their occupation of Norway during World War II.

How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love? | How Can I Tell If I'm Really In Love?  is an educational video released in 1986, and then again in 1992 in a classroom version.  It stars Jason Bateman, Justine Bateman, and Ted Danson.  It's based on a comic book of the same name by Sol Gordon, who also appears in clips in the video giving a lecture to students.  The video is intended to give teenagers straight clear answers about love and sex in a way they can relate to.  It includes interviews of students from University High School talking about their experiences with love and sex, as well as monologues and advice from Danson and the Batemans, and a music video.  The extremely 80s hair and clothing styles, and the bizarre editing and graphics make it often referenced as an accidental comedy.

Disconnect (film) | Disconnect is a 2012 American drama film directed by Henry Alex Rubin and stars an ensemble cast, which includes Jason Bateman, Hope Davis, Frank Grillo, Andrea Riseborough, Paula Patton, Michael Nyqvist, Alexander Skarsgård, Max Thieriot and fashion designer Marc Jacobs in his debut acting role.  The film explores how people experience the negative sides of modern communication technology by following three interconnecting stories.

Jessie Bateman | Jessie Eliza Bateman (2 August 1877 – 14 November 1940) was an English stage actress.  Bateman began her career as a child actress.  After early success on tour in Shakespearean roles, she built her career both in London and foreign tours.  She had her greatest success in the early years of the 20th century, and her career spanned over half a century.

visible_gold_support.title: Jan Troell
visible_gold_support.sentence: Jan Gustaf Troell (born 23 July 1931) is a Swedish film director.

- not add facts from gold documents that are absent from the actual inputs;
- not use or mention the gold answer.
